name: Catalan Shredded Salt Cod Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 small Red or green bell pepper, Prepared
1/2 small Bunch oregano leaves - finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
To prepare the salt cod, rinse it well and then immerse in a container of cold water (cut into chunks if necessary). Refrigerate, changing the soaking water 5 or 6 times until softened and mild (24 to 48 hours, depending on how stiff and salty it was initially).
Drain the cod. Place in a large saucepan with onion and bay leaves; cover with cold water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to very low, and simmer. Taste water; if too salty, drain, replace with fresh cold water, and bring to a boil. Simmer gently until fish is opaque, plumped, and falls apart into flakes (about 20 minutes).
Drain, pat dry, remove bones and skin if needed. Flake the cod.
Drain and press cod to extract moisture. Shred into thin strips and add to a large bowl.
Core, seed, and wash pepper; cut into thin rings. Separate rings of onion. Select 1 large, ripe tomato; slice thinly.
Add pepper rings, onion, and tomato to the bowl; gently toss with salad.
In another bowl, combine olive oil, vinegar, garlic, pepper, and salt (if needed). Whisk until blended.
Toss salad with vinaigrette. Transfer to platter and garnish with olives and egg wedges. Yields 6 servings
